title: Upgrade, CONNECT, Tunnels, WebSocket Boundaries, and Safe Transitions · Runtime Contract: Connect Authority Policy
summary: A client sends new-protocol bytes before acceptance, a server parses tunneled data as another HTTP request, buffered bytes disappear during handoff, and rejected transitions desynchronize the connection. Planned artifact: Add and verify the connect authority policy increment in confirmed protocol-transition and buffered-byte handoff state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse CONNECT authority-form, validate host and port policy, authenticate proxy use, establish the target, then transition after a successful response.
Validate Connection upgrade, Upgrade tokens, request completion, status 101, selected protocol, and ownership transfer before switching parsers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Connect Authority Policy · Theory Connect Authority Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 6238 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to wait for confirmed Upgrade or CONNECT acceptance before transferring parser ownership; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Connect Authority Policy contract for the supplied data.
